project.title: Chunxi Road Spatiotemporal Intelligence Cockpit
project.subtitle: Phase 4 dashboard closure on top of the unified warehouse and standardized experiment bundles.

metrics.label: Wide-table rows
metrics.detail: Rows currently exposed through the unified multimodal feature table.

metrics.label: Model-ready signal window
metrics.detail: Stable hours in the accepted forecast slice from 2025-01-11T05:00:00 to 2025-03-14T23:00:00.

metrics.label: Social join coverage
metrics.detail: Wide-table rows that carry non-null social features after hourly broadcast alignment.

metrics.label: Peak aligned video hour
metrics.detail: 7,205 detections in the aligned warehouse video table.

metrics.label: Road feature grids
metrics.detail: Shared-grid cells with non-zero road coverage in the static context table.

metrics.label: Aligned social days
metrics.detail: Daily social signals expanded into hourly rows and broadcast to the shared grid.

pillars.title: Unified Warehouse
pillars.subtitle: Phase 1 and 2 shared data base
pillars.description: The dashboard now reads the shared grid registry, cleaned modal tables, and multimodal feature table from the standardized warehouse instead of scattered module outputs.

pillars.title: Experiment Bundle
pillars.subtitle: Phase 3 standardized training outputs
pillars.description: Forecast evidence now comes from experiment_log.json plus checkpoint metadata inside analysis_outputs/experiments and analysis_outputs/checkpoints.

pillars.title: Decision Cockpit
pillars.subtitle: Phase 4 front-end closure
pillars.description: The frontend becomes a thin view layer over the warehouse and experiment bundles, which makes downstream deployment and validation much easier.

datasets.name: Shared Grid Registry
datasets.description: All aligned modal tables now join through the shared 105-cell Chunxi Road grid registry rather than module-specific grid logic.

datasets.name: Multimodal Feature Table
datasets.description: The Phase 2 wide table remains the single model-loading surface for training, validation, and dashboard summaries.

datasets.name: Signal Backbone
datasets.description: Signal data remains the prediction target and skeleton for the multimodal join, with legacy workweek and guoqing fallback already registered into DuckDB.

datasets.name: Aligned Video Hourly Table
datasets.description: Video is now spatially aligned to the shared grid and exposed through the warehouse, but its temporal overlap with the signal modeling slice is still narrow.

datasets.name: Standardized Experiment Bundle
datasets.description: The dashboard now reads formal experiment logs and checkpoint bundles instead of the legacy multimodal_hybrid folder.

datasets.name: Static Road Context
datasets.description: Road and subway proximity features are already broadcast to every signal row through the warehouse and do not need any extra frontend path wiring.

summary.note: The standardized Phase 4 warehouse does not yet register an OD corridor table, so this panel is intentionally left empty.

video.labelNote: Phase 4 now reads the aligned hourly warehouse table instead of raw trajectory files. Exact camera markers are omitted until the camera registry is promoted into the shared warehouse.

samples.title: Daily values are expanded to hourly rows
samples.theme_primary: 345 aligned days in the unified warehouse

samples.title: Comment volume stays as a separate feature channel
samples.theme_primary: 658.4K comments joined into the wide table

samples.title: Location precision is still coarse
samples.theme_primary: Regional broadcast instead of POI-level geocoding

streetScene.note: Street-view columns remain placeholder NULLs in Phase 4 until the Baidu API workflow is approved and registered into the shared warehouse.

summary.comparisonNote: The chart compares formal Phase 3 runs that share the same stable time filter; the accepted baseline remains the reference dashboard run.

timeline.note: This chart compares formal Phase 3 runs that share the same stable 2025-01-11 to 2025-03-14 training window and standardized experiment logging.

summaryCards.label: Accepted run
summaryCards.detail: Experiment 20260408_225928_chunxi_multimodal_base_hybrid from the standardized Phase 3 experiment bundle.

summaryCards.label: Test MAE
summaryCards.detail: Test RMSE 12.76 on the held-out stable slice.

summaryCards.label: Test WAPE
summaryCards.detail: Weighted absolute percentage error is the primary ratio metric after the Phase 3 update.

summaryCards.label: Comparable variants
summaryCards.detail: Formal runs with the same time filter and experiment family now feed the dashboard comparison panel.

highlights.title: Signal history still dominates the accepted multimodal run
highlights.detail: The test gate assigns dynamic 46.4%, ahead of video 13.2%, social 15.2%, and static 25.2%.

highlights.title: The hardest held-out slice lands in Zone D
highlights.detail: Zone D reaches test MAE 8.37 and WAPE 74.67% in the standardized experiment log.

highlights.title: Late-day hours remain the hardest forecasting regime
highlights.detail: Hour 16:00 has the highest test MAE at 10.36, while hour 04:00 stays lowest at 1.16.

highlights.title: The log1p exploratory run did not beat the accepted baseline
highlights.detail: Phase 4 compares only standardized runs with the same stable time filter; the baseline remains preferred because it keeps the lowest formal test error.

hybridTopGrids.label: Grid 7_2
hybridTopGrids.description: Within the accepted test window, this grid accumulates 6,621.0 observed signals and stays in Zone D.

hybridTopGrids.label: Grid 5_9
hybridTopGrids.description: Within the accepted test window, this grid accumulates 6,239.0 observed signals and stays in Zone A.

hybridTopGrids.label: Grid 9_12

hybridTopGrids.label: Grid 8_9
hybridTopGrids.description: Within the accepted test window, this grid accumulates 4,713.0 observed signals and stays in Zone C.

patterns.title: The dashboard now follows the warehouse instead of raw module folders
patterns.evidence: DuckDB already exposes cleaned and feature schemas, and the wide table contains 113,683 joined rows.
patterns.implication: Frontend deployment is now decoupled from scattered pipeline paths and can be validated against one warehouse contract.

patterns.title: Signal remains the model backbone
patterns.evidence: The model-ready signal slice spans 1507 hours with a peak of 4,042 signals.
patterns.implication: Future accuracy gains still depend first on keeping the signal backbone clean and temporally stable.

patterns.title: Video is aligned spatially but still sparse in time
patterns.evidence: The warehouse video table covers 9 hours across 7 grids, and the wide-table summary still reports 0.0% joined coverage.
patterns.implication: The Phase 4 frontend can already visualize the aligned video layer, but better overlap with signal hours is still needed for model lift.

patterns.title: Social coverage is broad but semantically coarse
patterns.evidence: The social warehouse layer contributes 345 aligned days and joins into 44.4% of the feature rows.
patterns.implication: This layer is useful for context and narrative cues, yet still needs POI-level geocoding before it becomes a strong explanatory signal.

patterns.title: Static road context is always available
patterns.evidence: Road features are non-null for 100% of feature rows, with 47 pedestrian-supporting grids already registered.
patterns.implication: Static context can anchor interpretability even when temporal external modalities are sparse or misaligned.

patterns.title: The accepted baseline is still signal-dominant
patterns.evidence: No formal exploratory variant has beaten the accepted stable-window baseline in the standardized experiment bundle.
patterns.implication: For now, the dashboard should present multimodal evidence as supportive context around a signal-led predictor rather than claim a strong multimodal lift.

patterns.title: Top hotspot cells stay concentrated in the retail core
patterns.evidence: Grid 7_2 leads the model-ready slice with 10,814.0 observed signals and a dominant evening period.
patterns.implication: Operational attention, interpretability views, and future intervention design should continue to prioritize the Chunxi retail spine.

chatPrompts: What changed in Phase 4 after the dashboard moved onto the unified warehouse?
Which modal tables are already in DuckDB and which ones are still sparse?
Why is the accepted forecast baseline still signal-dominant?
How much social coverage is actually landing in the multimodal feature table?
What does the aligned hourly video table tell us right now?
Which grids remain the hottest in the model-ready signal slice?
